cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out if following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. I need to go step by step and track my direction and position. Let's assume I start facing a certain direction, maybe north, just to have a reference. First instruction: Turn around. If I start facing north, turning around would make me face south. Second: Turn left. From south, turning left (which would be to the east direction if I'm facing south? Wait, no—if you're facing south and turn left, you turn 90 degrees to the east? Wait, no, if you're facing south and turn left, you would face east? Wait, actually, when you turn left, your left relative to your current direction. So if facing south, left turn would be to the east? Wait, no, let me think again. Imagine facing north. Turning left would be west, right? So if facing south, turning left would be east? Because if you're facing south, left is to your left, which would be east. Yes, that's right. So after turning around (facing south), then turning left, I'm facing east. Third: Take 1 step. So moving 1 step east. Fourth: Take 2 steps. Still facing east, so moving 2 more steps east. So total of 3 steps east from
